notes: With-skill pass_rate is slightly lower (-3.6%) entirely due to eval 3 (partial-area) where the skill correctly omitted findings for well-configured areas (Hooks, MCP) but the assertion demands explicit findings. This is an eval design issue, not a skill quality issue.
Baseline failed on eval 1 'Source URL' assertion because MCP returns relative paths — the skill explicitly formats full docs.anthropic.com URLs, which is a genuine structural improvement.

With-skill produces 5-7 focused findings per audit vs 15-22 from baseline. The adversarial reviewer filters ~65% of findings. Both versions converge on the same top-3 priorities (missing CLAUDE.md, stale permissions, no shared settings).
Token usage is ~5% lower with-skill (99k vs 105k mean) despite reading 5 specialist files + 1 reference file. The structured workflow avoids redundant exploration that baseline agents perform.
Time is comparable (~203s with-skill vs ~219s without), with baseline showing higher variance (stddev 39s vs 8s). The skill provides more consistent execution time.

Eval 3 baseline scored 100% but took the longest of any run (257s, 57 tool calls) — verbose output satisfies structural assertions but the report quality (16 findings, many weak) is lower than the focused 5-finding with-skill version.
